Document:
# Elves
Although superficially similar to Humans and Dwarves, Elves are easily identified by their pointy ears. They also come in many more colors.
## Cantrips All Elves start with an [color=#1e90ff]abridged grimoire[/color] containing a few simple magical spells that help with survival. You may select two to memorize for the duration of the shift. Once you have read from this book, it can generally be discarded; others cannot read your grimoire or study from it. (If you have the Magical Encounter background, there are rumors that Wizarding Federation agents sometimes have access to physical [color=#ff20ff]WizCoin[/color]s, which may be a means to mastering more of your grimoire...)
## Damage The body of an Elf is attuned to magic, not bruality. As a result they take [color=#ffa500]10% more damage from Blunt[/color] but [color=#1e90ff]25% less damage from Poison and Cellular decay[/color].
## Origins
Elves can come from a variety of worlds and have many different cultural backgrounds, but are usually lumped into three broad categories: High Elves, who aspire to pure thoughts and deeds; Dark Elves, who are ruthlessly pragmatic if not downright militaristic; and Wood Elves, who prize their forest homes above any particular ideology.
The native language of the Elves is commonly called Aielic, though it may have different names on different planets. Most Elves can also understand Sylvan (the language of Dionae and other treefolk) and Darktongue (a language of malign power.) They are not usually familiar with Sol Common.
It is rumored that there is an organization of Elves somewhere in distant space, called MAGE, which opposes the reckless showboating of the Human-run Wizarding Federation, but whether this organization is real or has any actual power is a matter of pure speculation.
